(born January 4, 1956)[[1]](#cite_note-wsj-1) is an American guitarist and composer. He has been a guitarist for the band [Wilco](/wiki/Wilco "Wilco") since 2004.

["100 Greatest Guitarists of All Time"](https://www.rollingstone.com/music/music-lists/100-greatest-guitarists-153675/eddie-hazel-43560/). *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*. Retrieved May 26, 2012.

Hoinski, Michael (May 8, 2009). ["Nels Cline on Masturbation, Thurston Moore, and Wilco's New Record"](https://web.archive.org/web/20090510231754/http://blogs.villagevoice.com/music/archives/2009/05/interview_nels.php). *The Village Voice*. Archived from [the original](http://blogs.villagevoice.com/music/archives/2009/05/interview_nels.php) on May 10, 2009. Retrieved June 1, 2009.

Musical artist

**Nels Courtney Cline** (born January 4, 1956)[[1]](#cite_note-wsj-1) is an American guitarist and composer. He has been a guitarist for the band [Wilco](/wiki/Wilco "Wilco") since 2004.

In the 1980s he played [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz"), often in collaboration with his twin brother [Alex](/wiki/Alex_Cline "Alex Cline"), a percussionist. He has worked with musicians in [punk](/wiki/Punk_rock "Punk rock") and [alternative rock](/wiki/Alternative_rock "Alternative rock") such as [Carla Bozulich](/wiki/Carla_Bozulich "Carla Bozulich") and the [Geraldine Fibbers](/wiki/Geraldine_Fibbers "Geraldine Fibbers"), [Mike Watt](/wiki/Mike_Watt "Mike Watt") and [Thurston Moore](/wiki/Thurston_Moore "Thurston Moore"). He leads the Nels Cline Singers, Nels Cline Trio, and the Nels Cline 4.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Cline was named the 82nd greatest guitarist of all time by *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* magazine in November 2011.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nels_Cline&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/95/Nils_double_neck_Wilco_London_on_24.2.10.jpg/250px-Nils_double_neck_Wilco_London_on_24.2.10.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nils_double_neck_Wilco_London_on_24.2.10.jpg)

Cline playing a double-necked guitar with Wilco in 2010

Cline began to play guitar at age 12 when his twin brother Alex Cline started playing drums. The brothers developed together musically, playing in a rock band called Homogenized Goo.[[3]](#cite_note-Village_Voice-3) Both graduated from [University High School](/wiki/University_High_School_(Los_Angeles,_California) "University High School (Los Angeles, California)"). Cline cites hearing a recording of [Jimi Hendrix](/wiki/Jimi_Hendrix "Jimi Hendrix") performing "[Manic Depression](/wiki/Manic_Depression_(song) "Manic Depression (song)")" as a defining moment in his decision to become a guitarist.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/3/38/Nels-Cline_DSC00309.jpg/250px-Nels-Cline_DSC00309.jpg)](/wiki/File:Nels-Cline_DSC00309.jpg)

Cline in Aarhus Denmark 2014 playing with the BB&C Band

Cline has performed on over 150 albums in jazz, pop, rock, country, and experimental music. He was featured in a cover story by *[Guitar Player](/wiki/Guitar_Player "Guitar Player")* magazine for his work with the rock band Wilco.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Nels_Cline&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Cline is married to [Yuka Honda](/wiki/Yuka_Honda "Yuka Honda") of [Cibo Matto](/wiki/Cibo_Matto "Cibo Matto").[[1]](#cite_note-wsj-1)[[6]](#cite_note-gilbert-2011-6) Their wedding was in Honda's hometown in Japan in November 2010.[[1]](#cite_note-wsj-1)[[6]](#cite_note-gilbert-2011-6) They met through Mike Watt when he assembled the group [Floored by Four](/wiki/Floored_by_Four "Floored by Four").[[1]](#cite_note-wsj-1)[[7]](#cite_note-music-illuminati-7) Cline joined Honda as a guest guitarist in the [Yoko Ono Plastic Ono band](/wiki/Plastic_Ono_Band "Plastic Ono Band") for their tour in 2010.[[8]](#cite_note-latimes-8) Cline joined Honda and her band Cibo Matto at the 2015 Solid Sound Festival.[[9]](#cite_note-9)
